text:
Bulkers: VV Bulker values remained mostly stable across all bulker sectors. Newcastlemax and Capesize values drifted marginallylower while Handysize saw positive movement especially for resales.
Panamax Mont Blanc Hawk (81,600 DWT, 2017, Imabari) sold by Eiko Kisen for USD 32.8 mil, VV Value USD 31.1 mil.
Panamax Marinicki C (76,600 DWT, 2005, Imabari) sold by Chandris to Chinese buyers for USD 11.5 mil, VV Value USD 10.4 mil.
Supramax African Tern (58,300 DWT, 2013, Dalian COSCO KHI) sold by MUR Shipping for USD 19.8 mil, VV Value USD 20.1 mil.
Supramax Santa Rita (55,700 DWT, 2010, Mitsui Tamano) sold by Okouchi Kaiun to Greek buyers for USD 17.2 mil, VV Value USD 17.5 mil.
Handy BC Dalarna (34,800 DWT, Jul 2014, Zhong Chuan) sold by Bertling FH for USD 17.2 mil, VV Value USD 14.0 mil.
Tankers: VV S&P values were broadly stable on a one-week basis. Modern Aframax values saw slight softening while older tonnage continues to appreciate.
Suezmax Cosmo Sail (159,200 DWT, 2007, Hyundai Samho) sold by Taihu Shipping for USD 49.5 mil, VV Value USD 48.6 mil.
LR2 Seriana (110,000 DWT, Sep 2015, Sumitomo) sold to Great Eastern Shipping for USD 72.0 mil, VV Value USD 73.0 mil.
LR1 Cape Taura & Cape Tempest (73,600-73,700 DWT, 2007/2008, New Times Shipbuilding) sold en bloc to Perosea Shipping for USD 20.5 mil and USD 22.1 mil respectively, VV Values USD 20.5 mil and USD 21.2 mil.
MR2 Sandpiper Pacific (51,800 DWT, Jun 2013, HSG Sungdong Shipbuilding) sold by Pacific Carriers (Singapore) to Undisclosed for

MR2 Sandpiper Pacific (51,800 DWT, Jun 2013, HSG Sungdong Shipbuilding) sold by Pacific Carriers (Singapore) to Undisclosed for USD 32.0 mil, VV Value USD 31.7 mil.
Containers: No new sales this week, but demand remains high, with values still coming up for older Feedermaxes.

Bulkers: Panamax values rose in response to high prices achieved by last week's Mont Blanc Hawk and Marinicki C sales.Supramax values dipped in response to the soft pricing of the Ocean Bright.
Supramax Ocean Bright (56,000 DWT, 2013, Mitsui) sold by Ming Tai Shipping Corp DD Passed for USD 19.2 mil, VV Value USD 21.0 mil.
Handy Sun Grace (33,700 DWT, 2004, Oshima) sold by STX Sun Ace Shipping to Greek buyers for USD 7.25 mil, VV Value USD 8.0 mil.
Tankers: VV S&P values saw positive movement across older tonnage this week, with older VLCCs, Suezmaxes and MR2s all appreciating. Older MR2 values being the standout performer. Asset values for older VLCCs remain well supported, with the Lila Kochi sale landing against a backdrop of tight fleet supply and healthy tanker earnings.
VLCC Lila Kochi (314,000 DWT, Dec 2008, Kawasaki) sold to MSC for USD 79.0 mil, VV Value USD 74.01 mil.
VLCC Yamatogawa (302,500 DWT, Dec 2006, Kawasaki) sold SS/DD Due by Chiba Shipping to Chinese buyers for USD 61.5 mil, VV Value USD 62.33 mil.
Containers: The S&P market remains quiet and demand remains high, values are still nudging up for older Feedermaxes but no notable movements across any other sectors.
Feedermax Steen (801 TEU, 2008, Peters SB) sold DD due to Turkish Buyers for USD 10 mil, VV Value USD 10.3 mil.
